Alan Johnson Software Developer

I build practical web applications using modern JavaScript technologies. My work focuses on clean architecture, responsive interfaces, and reliable backend systems.

About

I am a Computer Science student at the College of Engineering Adoor, building software from idea to deployment. My work has focused on full-stack web applications, especially with the MERN stack and real-time features.

I learn through building things that matter, not by following tutorials alone. Each project has helped me improve how I structure code, design interfaces, and make systems reliable enough for real use.

Experience

Jan 2026 – Present

Full Stack Developer

Library Navigator
GitHub Live Site
  • Built and deployed a library management platform with reservation, seat tracking, and community chat features.
  • Designed REST APIs with Node.js and Express for resource management and allocation workflows.
  • Developed responsive React interfaces and deployed the frontend and backend on Vercel and Render.
2025

Frontend Developer

Zarevix
GitHub Live Site
  • Designed a production company website with a mobile-first, responsive interface.
  • Built reusable React components and improved loading performance across the experience.
  • Completed deployment setup and refined the site for a cleaner corporate presentation.
Oct 2025

Participant

NASA Space Apps Challenge
  • Worked with an international team to design a web-based prototype for an Earth science challenge in a 48-hour hackathon.

Featured Projects

Library Navigator

MongoDB React Node.js Express WebSocket Vercel Render

Problem

Library seat management was manual and hard to coordinate.

Solution

I built a product that supports authentication, book search, reservations, live seat availability, and community chat.

Architecture

The stack uses React on the frontend, Express and Node.js on the backend, and MongoDB for persistent data.

Challenge

Preventing duplicate reservations required careful state handling and reliable backend logic.

Lesson

I learned how real-time systems change the way interfaces and data updates need to be designed.

GitHub Live Demo

Zarevix

React Responsive Design CSS HTML5 Vercel

Problem

The client needed a polished corporate site that felt fast and reliable on every screen size.

Solution

I built a lightweight React experience with reusable components and a mobile-first layout.

Architecture

The site is structured around modular UI sections and simple, maintainable styles.

Challenge

Keeping the layout consistent across unusual screen ratios required careful responsive design.

Lesson

I learned that performance is shaped by both code structure and asset decisions.

GitHub Live Demo
